q: The fuel manifold DELIVERS metered fuel flow to the 30 fuel nozzles. What is the synonym for "delivers"?
The three-digit numeric function code is used to INDICATE the particular maintenance function. What is the synonym for "indicate"?
If the oil heating system is necessary, REFER to the related Service Bulletin. What can be used instead of "refer"?
'Day of discovery' means the calendar day that a malfunction was RECORDED in the maintenance log. What is another word for "recorded"?
Any situation that could POSSIBLY crush or penetrate the case of the beacon should be avoided. What is the synonym for "possibly"?
The tail bearing housing CONTAINS three speed probes configured to read LP turbine speed. Which one CANNOT be used for "contains"?
All calendar days are considered to run CONSECUTIVELY. What is the meaning of "consecutively"?
Park the aircraft on a FLAT surface. What is a synonym for "flat"?
If there is a short circuit, you will cause damage to the serviceable computer when you SWAP the units. Which verb can be used instead of "swap"?
The drag is normally approx. 40 lbf.in. The PERMITTED maximum is 107 lbf.in. What does "permitted" mean?
'Safe operation of aircraft in cold weather conditions RAISES specific problems.' Which phrasal verb could be used for "to raise"?
Additional maintenance task may be required DEPENDING on the failure modes. What does "depending" mean in this context?
The outside of extinguishers should be cleaned with cotton wipers, REMOVING grease and dust.
You must OBEY the wiring standard practices to ensure proper electrical fault isolation. What is the synonym for "obey"?
The emergency shutdown procedure must only be used when UNUSUAL conditions occur. What is the synonym for "unusual"?
Do a general visual INSPECTION of the forward avionic compartment. What is the synonym for "inspection"?
The maintenance data must be ACCURATE to ensure correct repair. What is the synonym for "accurate"?
You must ENSURE that all safety pins are installed before towing the aircraft. What is the synonym for "ensure"?
The hydraulic fluid must be COMPATIBLE with the system seals and components. What is the synonym for "compatible"?
The aircraft must be AIRWORTHY before it is released for flight. What is the synonym for "airworthy"?
The technician must VERIFY the part number before installation. What is the synonym for "verify"?
The procedure was REVISED to include the latest safety requirements. What is the synonym for "revised"?
The maintenance schedule SPECIFIES the inspection intervals for each component. What is the synonym for "specifies"?
It is MANDATORY to carry a first aid kit on all commercial flights. What is the synonym for "mandatory"?
The technician must DISCONNECT the battery before working on the electrical system. What is the synonym for "disconnect"?
The ADJACENT structure must be inspected for damage after a hard landing. What is the synonym for "adjacent"?
The corrosion was found to be SUPERFICIAL and did not affect structural integrity. What is the synonym for "superficial"?
The borescope probe provides ILLUMINATION inside the engine cavity. What is the synonym for "illumination"?
The maintenance engineer ANNOTATED the drawing to show the modification areas. What is the synonym for "annotated"?
The aircraft is PROHIBITED from flying with the emergency exit blocked. What is the synonym for "prohibited"?
The DEFECTIVE brake pad was replaced during the unscheduled maintenance check. What is the synonym for "defective"?
You must RETAIN the old part for inspection by the quality department. What is the synonym for "retain"?
The engineer must MONITOR the hydraulic pressure throughout the ground test. What is the synonym for "monitor"?
The structural damage was ASSESSED by the designated engineering authority. What is the synonym for "assessed"?
The component was found to be SERVICEABLE after the detailed inspection. What is the synonym for "serviceable"?
The repair was AUTHORIZED by the quality assurance department. What is the synonym for "authorized"?
The PROXIMITY of the fuel line to the ignition source posed a fire risk. What is the synonym for "proximity"?
The test CONFIRMED that the hydraulic pump was operating within limits. What is the synonym for "confirmed"?
The technician must ADHERE to the maintenance procedures without deviation. What is the synonym for "adhere to"?
The temperature EXCEEDED the red line limit, triggering the overheat warning. What is the synonym for "exceeded"?
The access panel was SECURED with the correct fasteners and lock wire. What is the synonym for "secured"?
The engineer DOCUMENTED all findings in the aircraft technical log. What is the synonym for "documented"?
The anti-corrosion treatment INHIBITS the formation of rust on the metal surface. What is the synonym for "inhibits"?
The fuel RESIDUE in the tank must be removed before any hot work. What is the synonym for "residue"?
The technician was COMPETENT to perform the complex avionics task. What is the synonym for "competent"?
The pressure DIFFERENTIAL across the fuel filter was within limits. What is the synonym for "differential"?
The airframe structure must WITHSTAND the design ultimate load. What is the synonym for "withstand"?
The PRELIMINARY inspection showed no visible damage to the exterior. What is the synonym for "preliminary"?
The maintenance interval was EXTENDED based on the service experience data. What is the synonym for "extended"?
The crack PROPAGATED along the grain boundary due to fatigue. What is the synonym for "propagated"?
If you find seal or shroud interference and you think it is "MINOR", the engine is serviceable. What is the OPPOSITE of "minor"?
If you do not repair the protection within 2 hours, apply a corrosion preventive oil as a "TEMPORARY" protection. The OPPOSITE of "temporary" is:
The wheel assembly has an "OUTER" wheel half assembly. What is the OPPOSITE of "outer"?
Both air conditioning packs are "OPERATIVE". "Operative" can be replaced with:
The "MANUAL" transfer may be performed. The OPPOSITE of "manual" is:
Inspect window for "ALLOWABLE" damage limits. "Allowable" means:
Do a "DETAILED" visual inspection of the two external power receptacles. The OPPOSITE of "detailed" is:
"POTENTIAL" consequences are that wires may become open circuit. "Potential" can be replaced with:

The oil was found to be CONTAMINATED with metal particles. The OPPOSITE of "contaminated" is:
The procedure was APPROVED by the design authority. The OPPOSITE of "approved" is:
The aircraft was PRESSURIZED to cabin altitude of 8000 feet. The OPPOSITE of "pressurized" is:
The MANDATORY inspection must be completed before the aircraft returns to service. The OPPOSITE of "mandatory" is:
The corrosion treatment PREVENTS further oxidation of the metal surface. The OPPOSITE of "prevents" is:
The fuel system was INTACT after the bird strike event. The OPPOSITE of "intact" is:
The maintenance record was COMPLETE before the aircraft was dispatched. The OPPOSITE of "complete" is:
The engine was SERVICEABLE after the compressor blade replacement. The OPPOSITE of "serviceable" is:
The aircraft was PARKED in the hangar for the duration of the C-check. The OPPOSITE of "parked" is:
The hydraulic system operated NORMALLY after the seal replacement. The OPPOSITE of "normally" is:
All INTERNAL aircraft components were checked during the scheduled inspection. The OPPOSITE of "internal" is:
This can cause "THERMAL" shock to the stressed parts. What does "thermal" mean?
All the inspections are visual "UNLESS" otherwise specified. "Unless" can be replaced with:
Do a general visual inspection of the "FORWARD" avionic compartment. What is the synonym of "forward"?
Do a general "VISUAL" inspection of the FWD and AFT passenger doors. "Visual" means:
If the pressure will "EXCEED" 20 PSI absolute, you must remove the oxygen equipment. What can be used instead of "exceed"?
To remove deep signs of corrosion, brush the part with an "ABRASIVE" material. "Abrasive" means:
opts: causing damage or wear by friction

q: Major sub-zones are IDENTIFIED by the tens of the major zones. What does "identify" mean?
The aircraft must be CERTIFIED before it enters revenue service. What does "certified" mean?
The structural repair manual provides GUIDANCE on acceptable repair methods. What does "guidance" mean?
The maintenance technician must CALIBRATE the torque wrench before use. What does "calibrate" mean?
The airworthiness directive is MANDATORY for all operators of the aircraft type. What does "mandatory" mean?
The LUBRICANT reduces friction between moving engine components. What does "lubricant" mean?
The aircraft underwent a STRUCTURAL survey after the tail strike incident. What does "structural" mean?
opts: relating to the framework of the aircraft

q: The oxygen system is PRESSURIZED to provide flow to the masks. What does "pressurized" mean?
The BORESCOPE inspection revealed a cracked turbine blade. What is a borescope?
opts: a flexible optical device for internal inspection

q: The landing gear RETRACTION was tested on jacks before the aircraft's first flight. What does "retraction" mean?
The AILERON provides roll control for the aircraft. What is an aileron?
opts: a moveable surface on the horizontal stabilizer
a moveable surface on the wing for roll control
a vertical surface on the fin
a high-lift device on the leading edge

q: The HYDRAULIC accumulator stores energy in the form of pressurized fluid. What is an accumulator?
opts: a device that generates hydraulic pressure
a device that stores pressurized fluid or gas
a device that measures fluid flow
a device that filters hydraulic fluid

q: The TRANSPONDER sends identification and altitude information to air traffic control. What is a transponder?
opts: a radio device that responds to radar interrogation

q: The SQUIB on the fire extinguisher initiates the discharge when activated. What is a squib?
opts: an explosive device used to activate the fire bottle

q: The TARE weight is the weight of the aircraft without fuel or payload. What does "tare weight" mean?
opts: the empty weight of the aircraft

q: The FATIGUE life of the component is expressed in flight cycles. What does "fatigue" mean in aviation context?
opts: weakening of metal due to repeated stress cycles

q: The DATUM line is used as a reference point for weight and balance calculations. What is a datum?
The aircraft's AIRWORTHINESS certificate was issued after the initial certification. What does "airworthiness" mean?
opts: the ability of the aircraft to perform well
the condition of being fit and safe to fly

q: The fuel CONTAMINATION was caused by water ingress during the refueling operation. What does "contamination" mean?
opts: the presence of unwanted substances in a material

"To sign off" a maintenance task means to:
opts: certify that the work has been completed satisfactorily

q: "To top up" a fluid level means to:
opts: add fluid to reach the correct level

q: "To bleed" an aircraft system means to:
opts: remove trapped air or excess pressure from the system

q: "To lock out" a circuit breaker means to:
opts: prevent it from being reset to ensure safety during maintenance

q: "To run up" an engine means to:
opts: stop the engine on the ground

q: "To check out" (a tool or equipment) means to:
opts: inspect and return it to stores

q: "To stand down" in maintenance context means to:
opts: suspend or stop an activity as directed

q: "To write up" a defect means to:
opts: record it formally in the maintenance log
refer it to the crew chief verbally

q: "To break out" new materials means to:
opts: open and issue new items from storage

q: The word "rotable" in a parts context refers to:

q: The term "shelf life" refers to:
opts: the maximum number of flight cycles

q: The term "traceability" in maintenance records means:

q: The word "bonding" in aircraft electrical context means:
opts: connecting components with conductors to equalize electrical potential

q: The word "earthing" (or grounding) means:

q: The word "squib" in a fire suppression system refers to:
opts: a small explosive device used to release the fire suppressant

q: The term "cold soak" refers to:

q: The word "nacelle" refers to:
opts: the structure surrounding and housing an engine

q: The term "inlet guide vanes" in an engine refers to:

q: The word "flap" refers to:
opts: a high-lift device on the wing trailing edge

q: The term "stall" in an aerodynamic context means:
opts: a loss of lift due to exceeding the critical angle of attack

q: "Ground spoilers" on an aircraft:

q: A "squib test" on a fire extinguisher system is performed to:

q: The term "pitch" in rotor context means:
opts: the rotational speed of the rotor

q: An "overrun" condition on an aircraft engine means:
opts: the engine has run for too many hours

q: The term "hard time" component means:
opts: a part that is difficult to remove

q: "On condition" maintenance means:
opts: maintenance performed only after a failure

q: The word "placard" on an aircraft refers to:
opts: a maintenance instruction in the AMM
a sign or label fixed to the aircraft providing instructions or warnings

q: The term "ferry flight" means:
opts: a flight to reposition an aircraft, sometimes under a special permit
a training flight for new pilots
a test flight after major maintenance

q: "Special inspection" in maintenance terminology refers to:
opts: an inspection required after a specific event such as a hard landing or lightning strike

q: "Turbine entry temperature" is measured to:
opts: monitor the temperature at the turbine inlet and prevent overheating

q: The term "hydraulic snubber" is used to:
opts: dampen pressure spikes and reduce vibration in hydraulic lines
control the direction of hydraulic flow

q: The word "discrepancy" in maintenance context most nearly means:
opts: an abnormality or defect requiring action

q: "Cycle counting" for aircraft components refers to:
opts: tracking the number of pressurization cycles or landing cycles for fatigue life management

q: The term "pitch trim" refers to:
opts: adjusting the aircraft's nose-up or nose-down attitude

q: "Ram air" refers to:
opts: compressed air from the engine compressor
ambient air forced into the aircraft by its forward motion

q: The word "pitting" on a metal surface indicates:
opts: small holes or cavities caused by corrosion

q: The term "avionics bay" refers to:
opts: a dedicated compartment housing electronic equipment
the equipment bay under the passenger floor

q: "Engine run-in" after overhaul means:
opts: a high-power test to check maximum thrust
a controlled ground running sequence to bed in new components
the first flight after engine change
an idle run to check for oil leaks

q: The term "airworthiness" means:
opts: the aircraft is on time for departure
the aircraft is in a safe and proper condition for flight
the aircraft has passed a visual inspection

q: "Critical maintenance task" refers to:
opts: any task taking more than 2 hours
a task whose incorrect performance could directly affect flight safety
a task performed during a heavy maintenance check

q: The term "load sheet" in aviation refers to:
opts: a document showing the distribution of load and the resulting centre of gravity of the aircraft
a structural load calculation for repairs

q: The word "penultimate" means:
"Aircraft release" in maintenance context means:
opts: the aircraft has been returned to service after maintenance
the aircraft has been dispatched with a known defect
the aircraft has been assigned a new registration

q: The term "dual inspection" requires:
opts: two separate inspections on different days
inspection by two independent qualified persons

q: The word "annotate" most nearly means:
opts: add notes or comments to a document

q: The term "phased inspection" means:
opts: inspecting one aircraft phase at a time
dividing a maintenance check into smaller packages performed at shorter intervals
a special inspection after a phase of flight

q: "Fail-safe" design means:
opts: a system that will not fail under any condition
a system designed so that failure of one component does not cause failure of the whole system
a system that automatically shuts down on failure
a system requiring manual override on failure

q: The word "supersede" most nearly means:
"Fuselage frame" refers to:
opts: the longitudinal structural member of the fuselage
a transverse structural ring that gives the fuselage its shape and transfers loads

q: The term "stringer" in aircraft structure refers to:
opts: a longitudinal structural member running along the length of the fuselage or wing

q: "Over-torque" of a fastener refers to:
opts: applying less than the specified torque
applying more than the specified torque, potentially damaging the fastener or structure
applying torque in the wrong direction
failing to use a torque wrench

q: "Safety margin" in engineering means:
opts: the minimum load that must be applied
the additional strength built into a structure beyond the expected maximum load
the time allowed for an inspection
the gap between two structural components

q: The word "nominal" most nearly means:
opts: as named / within normal expected range

q: "Certification basis" of an aircraft refers to:
opts: the location where the aircraft was certified
the airworthiness standards and regulations to which the aircraft was type certified

q: "Continued airworthiness" documentation refers to:
opts: documents generated only at initial delivery
documents and instructions needed to maintain the aircraft's airworthiness throughout its service life

"ETOPS" refers to:
The word "disposition" of a rejected part means:
opts: the decision on what to do with the rejected part (scrap, rework, return, etc.)

q: "Snag" in British aviation English means:
opts: a defect or fault on the aircraft

q: The word "expedite" in maintenance context most nearly means:
opts: complete or process something more quickly
